retry_reason: Failure (NULL) means no slave channel is available; channels may be freed later, so a retry could succeed.
contract_summary: This function is a get-wrapper that acquires a reference on a DMA slave channel via dma_get_any_slave_channel. It returns the channel to the caller with its dreq field set from the OF specifier args. The caller becomes owner of the channel reference and must release it via dma_release_channel when done. On failure (NULL returned), no reference is acquired; the caller may retry later if channels become available.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a DMA channel reference via dma_get_any_slave_channel and return it; the only additional logic is setting the dreq field from the OF specifier, which is minimal and directly related to channel setup.

check.reason: dma_get_any_slave_channel returns a dma_chan pointer with its refcount increased. The function returns that same chan, so the critical variable is the return value. The access path chan.device.ref.refcount correctly traces from the return value (chan) through the device, ref, to refcount. The type chain dma_chan--dma_device--kref--refcount_t is consistent with the struct definitions. Path segment count matches type count.
